title: prunus × utahensis
intro: Prunus × utahensis, also known as Utah apricot or apricot plum, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Prunus × utahensis include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water or a solvent, and can be used to treat various ailments.
